auth.loginDescription: Welcome back. Use your administrator account to access the dashboard.
auth.emailHelp: Use the administrator email that has been granted access.
auth.passwordHelp: Password must be at least 6 characters.

chat.title: Chat Playground
chat.pageDescription: An AI Elements-style chat surface powered by the current streaming backend endpoint.
chat.workspacePageDescription: Chat against a selected workspace to test agent and orchestration behavior with the same streaming surface.
chat.formDescription: Send a streaming request to `/api/chat/completions` using your current authenticated session.
chat.targetRequired: Select an agent team or direct agent before sending.
chat.workspaceHelp: Pick the workspace whose agents and orchestration runtime should answer this chat.
chat.workspaceRequired: Select a workspace before sending a workspace chat request.
chat.agentHelp: Route directly to this agent, bypassing workspace orchestration.
chat.modelHelp: Use any model identifier supported by the active provider.
chat.systemPromptHelp: Changing the model or system prompt starts a fresh thread on the next send.
chat.promptPlaceholder: Ask a question, request a summary, or sketch a plan...
chat.conversationDescription: Messages are rendered in a chat-first layout while still using the current backend stream contract.
chat.sidebarDescription: Browse previous conversations and jump back into an existing thread.
chat.settingsDescription: Tune the active model and assistant behavior before you send the next prompt.
chat.emptyDescription: The current backend already supports streaming and conversation IDs, so this view now behaves more like a real chat thread.
chat.continueDescription: New prompts continue the current conversation until you clear the thread or change the session settings.
chat.startDescription: The first prompt creates a conversation and stores the returned conversation ID for the next turns.
chat.threadConnectedDescription: The backend returned a conversation ID. New prompts will continue this thread.
chat.threadIdleDescription: No conversation ID yet. Your next prompt will start a fresh thread.
chat.outputDescription: Assistant output is streamed chunk by chunk from the backend.
chat.emptyState: No response yet. Submit a prompt to test the chat completions API.
chat.traceDescription: Inspect the run summary and individual orchestration steps for this assistant reply.
chat.traceEmpty: Select an assistant reply to inspect its execution trace.
chat.traceLoadFailed: Could not load the run trace.
chat.traceNoSteps: No steps were recorded for this run.
chat.traceFlowDescription: Trace the path the reply took across orchestrator and worker agents.
chat.traceStepSelected: Select any node in the flow to inspect its payload and result.

suggestions.greeting: Write a short Vietnamese greeting for a new user.
suggestions.summary: Summarize the latest frontend conventions in 5 bullets.
suggestions.plan: Draft a rollout plan for adding image upload to chat.

page.title: Settings
page.description: Configure platform-wide settings including public IP, domain routing, and Traefik integration.

publicIp.label: Public IP
publicIp.hint: The public IP address of this server. Used for DNS verification when adding custom domains.

baseDomain.label: Base Domain
baseDomain.hint: Wildcard subdomain root (e.g. example.com). Resources get auto-assigned as <name>.example.com.

wildcard.label: Wildcard Subdomain
wildcard.hint: Automatically assign a subdomain to each new resource using the base domain above.

traefikNetwork.label: Traefik Docker Network
traefikNetwork.hint: Docker network name that Traefik is attached to (e.g. bridge). Containers will be joined to this network so Traefik can route to them.

certResolver.label: Cert Resolver Name
certResolver.hint: The name of the Let's Encrypt cert resolver configured in Traefik (e.g. letsencrypt).

appDomain.label: App Domain
appDomain.hint: Public domain for the NetClaw app itself (e.g. app.example.com). Applied instantly via Traefik file provider.

appTLS.label: Enable HTTPS for App
appTLS.hint: Enable HTTPS with HTTP→HTTPS redirect. Requires a domain and Let's Encrypt resolver.

appBackendURL.label: App Backend URL
appBackendURL.hint: Internal URL Traefik uses to reach this app. Default: http://app:8080 (Docker Compose service name).

acmeEmail.label: Let's Encrypt Email
acmeEmail.hint: Email registered with Let's Encrypt for TLS certificate notifications. Saved to DB immediately — use Restart Traefik to apply.

restartTraefik.label: Restart Traefik
restartTraefik.hint: Apply the current ACME config and restart Traefik (~5s downtime).

baseDomains.title: Base Domains
baseDomains.description: Wildcard domains that can be used to auto-assign subdomains to resources.

domainsCard.title: Domain Management
domainsCard.description: Manage wildcard base domains, DNS onboarding, and shared ingress from a dedicated page.

page.title: Domains
page.description: Manage app ingress, wildcard base domains, and the DNS foundation used by resources.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: This area controls the shared domains that NetClaw and your deployed resources rely on.

baseDomains.wildcardHint: Resources can be attached as subdomains under {{domain}}.
baseDomains.manualHint: Wildcard is disabled. Add records manually when routing new subdomains.

port.empty: Configure a container port on the resource before adding a domain.

add.domainHint: Use a hostname like example.com. Do not include http:// or https://.
add.domainInvalid: Enter a valid domain like example.com without http:// or https://.
